libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Keep the existing JVM/native-image test setup that adds opens for java.base/java.lang, java.base/java.net, and java.base/sun.nio.ch to ALL-UNNAMED. Tests should explicitly initialize/use the Conscrypt provider, e.g. Conscrypt.checkAvailability(), Conscrypt.newProvider(), and SSLContext/Cipher/Signature APIs with that provider, rather than relying on Conscrypt being installed globally.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Conscrypt extracts and loads a bundled JNI library from META-INF/native; if the runtime temp directory is mounted noexec, tests may need -Dorg.conscrypt.native.workdir=<writable-executable-dir>, but this is not normally required on the Gradle/CI environment.
The uber artifact only bundles native libraries for its published OpenJDK platforms; coverage remains platform-sensitive, especially under Native Image.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed: conscrypt-openjdk-uber has no Maven dependencies and bundles the platform JNI libraries. Meaningful tests should preserve the existing Java module opens because Conscrypt reflects into JDK internals on modern JDKs.
